skill.name: asana-automation
skill.description: Automate Asana tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): tasks, projects, sections, teams, workspaces. Always search tools first for current schemas.
skill.summary: Automate Asana task management through MCP - create tasks, projects, sections, teams, and perform bulk operations via Composio's Rube platform.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is purely documentation (SKILL.md) with no executable code. All 65 static findings are false positives: backticks are markdown code formatting, the URL is the legitimate public Rube MCP endpoint, and references to API operations are standard Asana functions not reconnaissance. Safe for publication.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Backtick Detection
high_findings.description: Static scanner detected backticks as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' but these are markdown code formatting (e.g., `ASANA_GET_MULTIPLE_WORKSPACES`). No executable code exists in this skill.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill contains only markdown documentation. Backticks are used for inline code formatting in markdown, not shell execution.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' on words like 'description' and 'notes'. These are normal text fields, not cryptographic code.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Text analysis shows these are ordinary field names in documentation, not cryptographic algorithms.

high_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged tool names like ASANA_SEARCH_TASKS_IN_WORKSPACE as 'system reconnaissance'. These are legitimate Asana API operations for task management.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Tool names reference Asana's documented API endpoints for standard productivity operations, not security reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: This skill enables Claude Code to manage Asana tasks, projects, sections, teams, and workspaces through Composio's Rube MCP, eliminating manual API interactions and enabling bulk task automation.
actual_capabilities: Create, search, list, and organize Asana tasks and subtasks
Manage Asana projects and sections with full CRUD operations
List and manage teams and workspace users
Perform bulk operations via parallel API requests
Resolve workspace and project names to GIDs for seamless integration
limitations: Requires Rube MCP connection via Composio platform
OAuth authentication must be completed through Asana
Operations are scoped to user-authorized workspaces only
Parallel requests do not support transaction rollback on failure

use_cases.title: Project manager automates recurring tasks
use_cases.description: A project manager creates weekly status update tasks, recurring sprint planning tasks, and automates task assignments across team members in Asana.

use_cases.title: Developer automates sprint workflows
use_cases.description: A developer uses Claude to automatically create task templates for code reviews, bug tracking, and deployment checklists at sprint start.

use_cases.title: Team automates bulk administrative tasks
use_cases.description: A team lead performs bulk operations like moving multiple tasks to sections, creating project duplicates, or updating assignees across many tasks at once.

prompt_templates.title: Create a new task
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the asana-automation skill to create a new task named [TASK_NAME] in the [PROJECT_NAME] project. Set the assignee to [EMAIL] and due date to [DATE].
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick task creation with basic details

prompt_templates.title: List project tasks
prompt_templates.prompt: Using asana-automation, list all tasks in the [PROJECT_NAME] project. Show task names, assignees, and due dates.
prompt_templates.scenario: View all tasks in a specific project

prompt_templates.title: Create project with sections
prompt_templates.prompt: Use asana-automation to create a new project called [PROJECT_NAME] in workspace [WORKSPACE]. Add sections for [SECTION_1], [SECTION_2], and [SECTION_3].
prompt_templates.scenario: Set up a new project with organized sections

prompt_templates.title: Bulk create tasks
prompt_templates.prompt: Using asana-automation, create these tasks in bulk: [TASK_LIST]. Each task should be added to project [PROJECT_NAME] with the appropriate assignee.

output_examples.input: Create a task called 'Review PR #123' in the 'Engineering' project, assign to john@example.com, due tomorrow
output_examples.output:
Task created successfully!
- Task Name: Review PR #123
- Project: Engineering
- Assignee: john@example.com
- Due Date: 2026-02-25
- Task GID: 1234567890

output_examples.input: List all tasks in the 'Q1 Sprint' project
output_examples.output:
Found 12 tasks in 'Q1 Sprint':
1. Setup CI/CD - @alice - Due Feb 28
2. Write API docs - @bob - Due Mar 1
3. Code review - @carol - Due Feb 26
...

output_examples.input: Create a new section called 'In Review' in the 'Engineering' project
output_examples.output:
Section created successfully!
- Name: In Review
- Project: Engineering
- Section GID: 0987654321

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before use
Verify Asana connection status shows ACTIVE before running workflows
Use workspace GID resolution pattern to convert names to IDs for API calls
anti_patterns: Assuming tasks exist without checking - always verify task GIDs returned from creation calls
Skipping workspace GID resolution - most operations require workspace context
Not handling pagination for large result sets - check next_page in responses

faq.question: What is Rube MCP?
faq.answer: Rube MCP is Composio's Model Context Protocol server that provides integration with Asana and other services. It handles the OAuth flow and API communication.

faq.question: Do I need an Asana API key?
faq.answer: No. Rube MCP uses OAuth authentication. You connect your Asana account through the Rube interface, and it handles authentication automatically.

faq.question: Can I access any Asana workspace?
faq.answer: You can only access workspaces that you authorize during the OAuth connection process. The skill operates within your authorized permissions.

faq.question: How do I find workspace and project GIDs?
faq.answer: Use the ID resolution patterns in the skill: call ASANA_GET_MULTIPLE_WORKSPACES to find workspace GIDs, and ASANA_GET_WORKSPACE_PROJECTS to find project GIDs.

faq.question: What happens if a bulk operation partially fails?
faq.answer: Individual requests in parallel operations are independent. If one fails, others continue. There is no automatic rollback - you must handle failure recovery manually.

faq.question: Can I create recurring tasks?
faq.answer: The skill does not have native recurring task support. You would need to create tasks individually or use Asana's built-in recurrence feature separately.
